patterns.label: Azure DevOps Work-Item Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: 30 ADO notification emails/week matching Task/Requirement/Bug/Feature/Epic subject patterns. Subscriber-only role means zero judgment required — pure rule-based triage yields the highest automation score (95) in catalog. Volume consistent since cycle 12.

patterns.label: Recurring Meeting Agenda Generator
patterns.llmRationale: 6 recurring weekly meeting types with fixed time slots and stable participant sets: Evals Sync (Tue 3:05), Project Crucible v-Team (Mon 8:05), Manager Team Meeting (Mon 11:05), Solution Library (Mon 9:05), Learning Fridays (Fri 2:05), Golden Evals bi-weekly (Thu 2:05). All extended through August. Agenda scaffolding fully templatable per meeting archetype.

patterns.label: Eval Coaching, Scoping & Expert Q&A
patterns.llmRationale: 15 weekly occurrences across 5 participants blocked on one PM eval expert. Recurring questions: how many eval cases are enough?, what metrics to use (groundedness, Compare Meaning, tool_call_accuracy)?, when in the agent lifecycle should evals happen?, CLI vs in-product tooling tradeoffs. Rising as field delivery scales and EvalCon April 2 deadline approaches.

patterns.label: Meeting Notes & Action Item Capture Pipeline
patterns.llmRationale: Repeating tri-phase workflow confirmed 8x/week: meeting scheduled → Loop doc created (Agenda, Notes, Decisions, Action Items, Open Questions) → async follow-up. Appears across multiple months and meeting types — a fully stable pattern with predictable structure enabling near-end-to-end automation.

patterns.label: External Partner Eval Session Follow-Up Drafter
patterns.llmRationale: Largest time block in dataset: 6 external partner sessions/week across 50+ domains (Accenture 2h, KPMG groups 3h, EY 55m, HCLTech 1h50m, Avanade 1h, Cognizant 1h) totaling 13+ hours. Structured follow-up format is highly templatable across known SI and enterprise customer partner archetypes. Rising as customer eval program expands.

patterns.label: Eval Results Analysis & Strategy Reporting
patterns.llmRationale: 8 cross-surface occurrences/week across 6 participants: CAPE/Evals leadership strategy threads, EvalCon content planning, Project Crucible evals-first cross-org alignment. EvalCon April 2 deadline drives elevated synthesis demand. Rising trend as strategic reporting surface area grows.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Artifact Recreation (Dedup Gap)
patterns.llmRationale: 5 team members independently creating eval checklists, Excel templates, scorecards, and enablement decks. Meetings explicitly flag multiple floating versions needing consolidation: CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, TFM Agent Evals, Evaluation.pptx, eval convo decks. A canonical scaffold skill eliminates cross-role duplication.

patterns.label: Broadcast & Digest Email Classifier
patterns.llmRationale: 12 broadcast/digest emails/week: Security/Compliance/Purview alerts (7×) and Viva Engage digests (5×). Zero judgment required — classify-and-route only on subject/sender vocabulary patterns. High automation ceiling offset by limited strategic value to PM.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Report Generation
patterns.llmRationale: Same done/next/blockers content reformatted every week across Loop, slides, and email: weekly update, bi-weekly newsletter, Cape Day/LT slides. Teams asked repeatedly to restate progress in different artifacts for the same underlying content. Single canonical input → all format variants eliminates reformatting churn.

patterns.label: PRD, Spec & Dashboard Artifact Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: 6 document events/week (ZIP packages with SKILL.md ×5, HTML dashboard ×1) following fixed schema structures. Recurring loop: signal ingestion → pattern detection → dashboard refresh → refinement notes. ZIP packages produced at short intervals suggest a high-cadence spec-and-prototype cycle amenable to scaffolding automation.

patterns.label: Cross-Org VOC & DRI Routing Assistant
patterns.llmRationale: PM explains VOC routing and DRI ownership for eval-related asks 4×/week, functioning as an informal routing layer between field engineers, PM, and PG teams. Routing logic is stable and encodable from known org chart DRI assignments and standard team structures.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution Hub
patterns.llmRationale: 8 Teams artifact-sharing events/week (GitHub repos, SharePoint ZIPs, Loop components, YAML templates, markdown specs, aka.ms links) with PM as canonical link hub. Claude can maintain a living artifact index tracking recency, context, and access paths — reducing repeat lookups.

patterns.label: High-Urgency Escalation Email Drafter
patterns.llmRationale: NEW in cycle 44 tracking. PM switches into a direct escalation style when something blocks progress: URGENT Degree CANNOT be Located (external university) and URGENT DBS Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites. Consistent structure: URGENT prefix, direct tone, specific blocking ask, external or senior cross-org audience. Rising trend.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture & MCP/Skills Scoping Advisor
patterns.llmRationale: Richest technical thread in dataset: MCP servers, CLI vs UI tradeoffs, WorkIQ integration constraints, skills architecture (skill-creator, skills-detective, PRD generator), Copilot Studio vs SDK decisions. High value despite moderate automation ceiling due to architectural complexity. Rising trend as agentic platform matures.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning Assistant
patterns.llmRationale: NEW this cycle. Files like [Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day indicate a repeat personal cadence — PM updates a structured planning artifact daily rather than starting from scratch. High automation ceiling: predictable morning trigger, consistent calendar + inbox context sources, stable planning output format.

patterns.label: Stakeholder 1:1 Meeting Prep & Follow-Up
patterns.llmRationale: 6 one-on-one stakeholder meetings/week totaling 3h 45m. Consistent pattern of pre-meeting agenda alignment and post-meeting action capture. Individual relationship context limits full automation but prep templates and follow-up drafts remain high-value for a recurring weekly load.

candidateSkills.name: recurring-agenda-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates structured agenda scaffolds for any of 6+ identified recurring meeting archetypes. Adapts content based on prior meeting context, recent blockers, and participant roles.
triggerExamples: Generate agenda for Tuesday Evals Sync
Prepare Monday Project Crucible v-Team agenda with last week's blockers
What should we cover in today's Solution Library check-in?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates manual agenda prep across 8+ weekly recurring meetings (5.75h/week), ensuring consistency and carrying forward open items automatically.

candidateSkills.name: eval-coaching-guide
candidateSkills.description: Answers recurring eval strategy questions about case depth, metric selection, lifecycle timing, and tooling differences. Encodes PM expert knowledge for self-service by field engineers and PMs.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases are enough for a production-ready Copilot agent?
When in the agent development lifecycle should I run evals?
What metrics should I use for a grounded RAG agent vs a tool-calling agent?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Removes 15 weekly escalations to a single eval expert across 5 participants — unblocking field delivery velocity without increasing PM load.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Triages Azure DevOps work-item notification emails by type, subscriber vs assignee role, and action required. Routes to appropriate queue, flags for action, or auto-dismisses noise.
triggerExamples: Triage my ADO notification emails from the last 24 hours
Which ADO items actually require my attention vs just informational?
Filter ADO emails where I am direct assignee vs just subscriber
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Automates 30 ADO notification emails/week at automation score 95 — highest in catalog. Reclaims 45 min/week of pure inbox noise processing.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Converts meeting transcripts or raw notes into structured Loop documents with Agenda summary, Decisions, Action Items with owners, Open Questions, and Follow-up tasks.
triggerExamples: Extract action items from this meeting transcript
Create Loop meeting notes from my Teams call notes
Structure these notes into decisions, owners, and follow-ups
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Automates the tri-phase post-meeting workflow confirmed 8x/week across 5 participants — saves ~70 min/week of manual Loop structuring.

candidateSkills.name: external-followup-drafter
candidateSkills.description: Drafts post-session follow-up emails and summary notes for external partner and customer eval sessions. Adapts tone and content depth based on partner type (SI, ISV, enterprise customer) and session outcomes.
triggerExamples: Draft follow-up email after the Accenture eval session today
Summarize the KPMG eval session and write a follow-up with next steps
Generate post-session notes and action items from today's external partner call
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Addresses the largest time block in the dataset — 13h/week of external partner sessions across 50+ domains — with structured, partner-calibrated follow-up drafts that scale without PM bottleneck.
